125442.explanation: We want to focus on citizen engagement by providing sufficient participation opportunities.

125445.explanation: We value all forms of agricultural entrepreneurship, both regular and innovative. We only do not want the number of tourists to get out of hand. The established development framework for recreational stays is leading here.

125446.explanation: We want to evaluate good ideas positively, thinking in terms of opportunities. This also applies to individuals’ ideas.

125448.explanation: Based on our Christian duty of charity, we are open to housing status holders and refugees within our municipal borders. Cooperation with neighboring municipalities and the province is essential.

125449.explanation: We do not want to make this mandatory, but if an application comes in, we want to approach it positively.

125453.explanation: If there is community demand for this, we want to cooperate. As a municipality, we also want to stimulate environmentally friendly alternatives, such as a light show.

125454.explanation: From 2027 onward, we will centrally collect residual waste at accessible and well-equipped locations. When the user pays, waste disposal remains affordable and fair.

125455.explanation: We want to freeze the number of visitors at existing large events, limit the closing time, and introduce a maximum decibel level as a noise standard. In addition, we cannot support events that do not align with the Christian identity.

125457.explanation: Yes, but always in close cooperation with the municipality.

125461.explanation: When sports clubs experience financial difficulties, they can apply for subsidies in consultation with the municipality.

125463.explanation: We advocate for an increase in the number of enforcement officers (boa’s) to support the police. Supervising and enforcing rules are core tasks of the boa’s.

125464.explanation: If necessary, we will install (temporary) cameras in locations where nuisance occurs to help prevent unsafe situations.

125469.explanation: Permanent residence in holiday parks conflicts in daily practice with recreational living.

125470.explanation: As a municipality, we need to look creatively at solving the housing shortage. Where possible, we want to realize courtyards and ribbon developments outside the main centers.

125471.explanation: Where possible, we want to encourage this.

125472.explanation: At the SGP, caring for one another is central. This means we will continue to support the most vulnerable in society.

125475.explanation: Where there is demand and possibilities, we want to create more charging options for electric cars.

125476.explanation: We believe it is important that space for identity-based care remains safeguarded.

125477.explanation: Prevention is better than cure. That is why we focus on prevention in youth care. Quality is prioritized over costs.

125478.explanation: We will only raise municipal taxes if there is a clear and legitimate reason to do so.

125480.explanation: The tourist tax was recently increased. We will only raise municipal taxes if there is a clear and legitimate reason to do so.

125481.explanation: We see Sunday as a day of rest given to us to serve God. We believe this day of rest is beneficial for all people; therefore, we are against shops opening on Sundays.

125482.explanation: We want to carry out this statutory task in cooperation with our neighboring municipalities and the Province.

125484.explanation: Agricultural land is invaluable for growing our agricultural products. We prefer to place solar panels primarily on roofs or on coverings of parking lots.

125488.explanation: We want to help our schools shape their cultural education by offering them materials for a lesson series about local culture.

125489.explanation: Due to the many issues involved, we are firmly against establishing a coffeeshop in Veere.

125490.explanation: We build according to need; therefore, we focus on a mix of housing in all centers to create a wide range of homes.

125491.explanation: We want to introduce the possibility of building multiple residential floors on the edges of the centers so we use the available space effectively. However, this requires a suitable location and well-considered landscape integration. High residential towers go too far for us.

125492.explanation: We want to use available living space as effectively as possible. This measure does not contribute to that.

125493.explanation: On the edges of the coastal centers, we want to create parking lots including toilet facilities where residents and tourists can park cheaply. This measure positively affects making the centers car-reduced and reduces parking nuisance in the non-coastal centers.

125496.explanation: Tourists are welcome in Veere. However, we do not want the number of tourists to get out of hand. Unrestricted growth is absolutely no longer an option. Quality takes precedence over quantity!

125497.explanation: In the context of the Northwest Route, we want to improve the existing asphalt and use the current road structures as effectively as possible. Building a (partially) new road is seen as an extreme last resort, which we definitely do not prefer.

125498.explanation: The needs may differ per center. Therefore, we focus on tailored solutions.

125499.explanation: This can vary per case. Therefore, we focus on tailored solutions.

125442.explanation: Involvement of residents is very important to us. See also our election program.

125445.explanation: Campsites must comply with different rules. Camping on a farmer's land is subject to other regulations.

125446.explanation: In the initial phase, the municipality should provide financial support, preferably together with other parties including the initiators. This concerns modest start-up costs.

125448.explanation: We want to take responsibility for our share of refugee reception. We must help people in great need. We have accommodated minors, welcome status holders, and many refugees from Ukraine are being accommodated. This is well coordinated in Zeeland.

125449.explanation: Housing for seasonal workers does not necessarily have to be on the company's own premises as far as we are concerned. Employers must provide assistance in this, and a combination of employment contract and rental contract is undesirable and can lead to abuses.

125453.explanation: Good for connectivity within the centers, reducing vandalism, and private fireworks use. See also our election program.

125454.explanation: The waste stream must be reduced in the Netherlands. The polluter pays principle is important in this, alongside other measures, including free disposal at the recycling center.

125455.explanation: Standards for festivals and parties are now in order. Enforcement needs more attention; without enforcement, standards are not very effective.

125457.explanation: It is not good for the open character of our flat country. However, consideration can be given to realizing permanent homes in barns that are now being converted into holiday homes, provided they are affordable.

125461.explanation: Physical activity in association strengthens livability. We invest in this. Associations can receive support.

125463.explanation: The municipality is the director in combating crime, nuisance, and intimidation. Boa’s play a central role here. See also our election program.

125464.explanation: People should be able to move freely. Cooperation in fighting crime starts with clear rules and enforcement, dialogue, and addressing each other. That way, we maintain balance in safety and privacy.

125469.explanation: Contributes to solving the housing shortage. However, it depends on the location of the holiday home.

125471.explanation: Encouraging people to participate in society counteracts loneliness and strengthens livability. Participation is not an obligation.

125472.explanation: If necessary, the municipality must support residents with a low income.

125475.explanation: The municipality promotes electric driving and facilitates charging opportunities.

125476.explanation: Care providers are open to different cultures and beliefs.

125477.explanation: Procurement of (youth) care must primarily be determined by what is needed and who can provide it well with a good price/quality ratio.

125478.explanation: The property tax (OZB) offers a fair way to keep municipal facilities affordable.

125480.explanation: The distribution of costs must remain fair. If there are higher expenses, tourists should also contribute. On the other hand, vacationing in Veere must remain affordable.

125481.explanation: That is up to the entrepreneurs themselves. See also our election program.

125482.explanation: We must solve the shortage of rental housing by building more affordable homes.

125484.explanation: On agricultural land less suitable for farming, this is possible, provided it is well integrated into the landscape. This means that not all of Walcheren will be covered.

125488.explanation: Dialect is part of our Veers cultural heritage and our identity. See also our election program.

125489.explanation: In the Netherlands, we have chosen to allow coffee shops under conditions. That policy also applies to Veere.

125490.explanation: For new construction, that is a good idea. This allows us to realize a reasonable waiting time for social rental housing. See also our election program.

125491.explanation: High residential towers do not fit in Veere. Adding one floor is acceptable in some places. The housing shortage must be resolved.

125492.explanation: Unclear statement. Is this about tourist rentals, room rentals for starters, or another explanation?

125493.explanation: There is a reasonable rate whereby parkers contribute to the facilities.

125496.explanation: We oppose increasing the number of sleeping places. The pressure from tourism is already high. More and more residents experience nuisance. We want to protect the peace, space, and nature in Veere. See also our election program.

125497.explanation: More asphalt is not the solution due to negative effects on the landscape, costs, and its attracting effect. We want to curb the growth of tourism. First, investigate how to better distribute traffic through a dynamic traffic guidance system. See also our election program.

125498.explanation: Size and problems differ per locality. Therefore, solutions and related costs also differ.

125499.explanation: Livable communities also need care facilities. See also our election program.

125442.explanation: There are sufficient other appropriate democratic instruments.

125445.explanation: Provided they meet the set conditions.

125446.explanation: Principle of equality: whether alone or together should not matter. Joint generation is often financially more advantageous than doing it alone.

125448.explanation: Also, the primary authority on this point does not lie with the municipal council.

125449.explanation: We do see the municipality's role as facilitating (thinking in terms of opportunities and possibilities).

125453.explanation: We do see the municipality's role as facilitating (thinking in terms of opportunities and possibilities).

125454.explanation: We do want waste collection centers to remain within an easily accessible distance for residents.

125461.explanation: Assess on a case-by-case basis what this sports club contributes to the well-being of our residents.

125464.explanation: There is sufficient supervision in our municipality.

125469.explanation: This enables the creation of permanent residential addresses despite the nitrogen issue.

125470.explanation: But the preference is for densification over expansion.

125471.explanation: This allows you to maintain a normal work rhythm and makes it easier to resume your career later. Moreover, the community may also expect some return for the money paid.

125472.explanation: Then the incentive for, for example, waste separation disappears for municipal waste charges. There are other methods to help these residents.

125475.explanation: As long as there is grid congestion, it is better to use the available electricity for building homes.

125476.explanation: Care is care, regardless of faith, culture, or belief.

125477.explanation: Quality is important, not the price.

125478.explanation: The property tax (OZB) in the municipality of Veere is already one of the lowest in the Netherlands.

125480.explanation: This has already been increased considerably in recent years.

125481.explanation: Sunday shopping contributes to vibrancy within our communities.

125482.explanation: We prefer to give priority to our residents and those with strong ties to the community.

125484.explanation: If there is a good opportunity somewhere that is not disruptive to the surroundings, then it should be possible. We think in terms of opportunities and possibilities.

125488.explanation: Dialect is cultural heritage that needs to be preserved.

125490.explanation: Building should not be linked to percentages; building is already difficult enough nowadays.

125491.explanation: Low-rise buildings are characteristic of our municipality and we want to keep it that way.

125492.explanation: Especially for students and seasonal workers, this can be a good solution. The quality of the rented property must, of course, be in good condition.

125493.explanation: Our residents consider parking convenience very important, and so do we.

125496.explanation: These must comply with the conditions set out in the recreational development framework.

125497.explanation: We strive to make use of existing asphalt as much as possible. Finding a suitable long-term solution is more important to us than having more or less asphalt.

125445.explanation: Provided that the conditions set in the policy are met.

125446.explanation: The role of the municipality should be to facilitate and encourage such initiatives.

125448.explanation: The municipality of Veere already contributes on a scale that fits our municipality and cooperates with Middelburg and Vlissingen.

125449.explanation: The Veerse VVD wants employers to have the possibility to provide good accommodation for their employees during seasonal work.

125453.explanation: The municipality of Veere should not do it, but it is possible.

125454.explanation: The polluter pays. Offering free service at the recycling center should remain possible.

125455.explanation: There are no problems with organized festivals in the municipality of Veere.

125457.explanation: We take good care of the beautiful countryside in the municipality of Veere. Housing construction is arranged within the new building programming.

125461.explanation: Every association can make use of the applicable schemes.

125463.explanation: The core task of BOAs (public enforcement officers) is enforcement, not safety. Safety can be an indirect result of enforcement.

125469.explanation: Only allow use as an informal care home.

125470.explanation: The housing assignment cannot be realized by infill alone.

125471.explanation: Not everyone receiving benefits is capable of performing socially valuable work. On the other hand, people who are capable can also work in regular jobs.

125472.explanation: Veerse residents who, due to circumstances beyond their control, have difficulty making ends meet can count on the support of the Veerse VVD.

125475.explanation: Charging stations are not a municipal task.

125476.explanation: The care people need must be provided.

125477.explanation: It is about the price-quality ratio.

125478.explanation: Taxes should not rise unnecessarily sharply.

125480.explanation: An increase has just been implemented and that is enough.

125484.explanation: It does not fit in our open landscape.

125488.explanation: Educational programs are not a municipal task.

125489.explanation: This does not fit in the DNA of the Veerse tourism portfolio.

125490.explanation: The housing programming must match the demand.

125491.explanation: High-rise apartments do not fit in the cherished Veerse landscape.

125493.explanation: Parking fees have been normalized and are up-to-date.

125496.explanation: In Veere, we aim for control over growth. Not blindly more of the same, but room for innovative and manageable plans that add value.

125497.explanation: The preference of the Veerse VVD is, of course, to make use of existing structures. However, the task of relieving our residents is so great that a limited addition of new asphalt is unavoidable.

125498.explanation: In every community, residents should have the same level of living enjoyment, livability, and safety. This means that some communities will require more investment than others. Tailored solutions are a must.

125442.explanation: The CDA finds it important that residents have a voice in important decisions. Ideally, they provide their input through the existing political parties. A referendum is then probably not necessary. This costs a lot of time and money.

125445.explanation: When we ask farmers to work more environmentally consciously, it is important to offer alternatives to earn a decent living: small-scale campsites and shops with self-produced items fit well here.

125446.explanation: Consider supporting energy cooperatives, such as covering parking lots with solar panels in which residents can participate via a postcode lottery.

125448.explanation: We believe that people in need should be sheltered. We opt for small-scale accommodation suitable for the size of our residential areas.

125449.explanation: We understand that seasonal workers also need housing somewhere, but the number of homes is already limited. Therefore, the preference is that employers themselves provide proper accommodation for their employees, provided it is well integrated spatially.

125453.explanation: Organizing fireworks shows is not a primary task of the municipality. However, the municipality can support safe and responsible initiatives from private individuals to maintain the tradition of fireworks on New Year's Eve.

125454.explanation: When residents have to take bulky waste to the recycling center themselves, it is a) difficult for people without a car; b) causes extra traffic nuisance; c) therefore results in no or very limited environmental gain; d) and increases the chance of illegal dumping.

125455.explanation: Permits can take into account other circumstances as well, such as the period and times of the event.

125457.explanation: The CDA considers good spatial planning important. The municipality must ensure that our beautiful landscape is not overbuilt.

125461.explanation: Sports are good for body and mind. Furthermore, it strengthens community spirit and prevents loneliness. That is worth investing in.

125463.explanation: We see that there are fewer and fewer police officers. Enforcement officers can help provide extra safety.

125464.explanation: Only if other means do not work and cameras are a last resort to achieve the intended goal.

125469.explanation: Especially Domburg summer homes in the centers are very suitable to be used as a permanent residence. Vacation parks, on the other hand, should only be used for holidays.

125470.explanation: We are careful with our beautiful landscape. Only when there is no more space in the village center will we look outside the existing village boundaries.

125471.explanation: We find it important that everyone participates in our society. We believe everyone is valuable and can contribute to a more beautiful Veere.

125472.explanation: The strongest shoulders bear the heaviest burdens. We must help people with a low income so they can build a good life. The hope is that they will eventually become financially independent.

125475.explanation: The municipality will only designate more charging points after thorough research shows a need in the area concerned.

125476.explanation: Healthcare is under pressure. Good care not only costs a lot of money, it also requires good staff. And that is often the problem: it is increasingly difficult to find enough people.

125477.explanation: Quality is more important than price. We want the best for our children. However, we do want to carefully consider what is really necessary.

125478.explanation: Residents also contribute to the facilities. We find facilities important, and therefore that is worth investing in.

125480.explanation: Tourist tax can only be increased when the municipality faces rising costs.

125481.explanation: The current number of shopping Sundays is fine.

125482.explanation: Refugees with residence permits follow the regular process for allocating social rental housing; the legislation is leading in this regard.

125484.explanation: The CDA considers roofs and parking lots much more suitable for solar panels than costly agricultural land.

125488.explanation: The CDA does consider it important that our children have knowledge of Zeeland culture and our heritage. Visiting museums and learning our history are part of this, but it is up to the school to shape the educational program.

125489.explanation: The CDA does not consider a coffeeshop in our municipality desirable.

125490.explanation: We find it important that a neighborhood consists of a mix of homes to promote meeting and cohesion. A balanced age distribution ensures a balanced use of facilities.

125491.explanation: High residential towers do not fit in the Zeeland landscape. To realize affordable homes, slightly taller apartment complexes can be built, fitting the scale of the village.

125492.explanation: More tourist beds are not desirable, but we do see an opportunity here to partly solve the housing problem.

125493.explanation: Paid parking is a way to have day tourists contribute to the level of facilities, such as public toilets and beach maintenance.

125496.explanation: It is important that houses come first for our own residents. Until the traffic problem is solved, no additional tourist beds will be added.

125497.explanation: New asphalt damages nature and our precious landscape. With some smart solutions, the worst traffic inconvenience can be eliminated. This also costs much less money.

125498.explanation: The CDA believes that every village has the right to a comparable level of facilities and the same quality and maintenance of public spaces.

125499.explanation: To allow elderly people to live longer near their familiar surroundings, it is important that several care locations are realized in Veere.

125442.explanation: D66 considers democracy and participation very important. Honest and transparent governance requires listening to residents.

125445.explanation: D66 likes to clear away unnecessary rules, think of opening hours. And as far as D66 is concerned, a café may sell a book, and a hairdresser may serve a glass of wine. Therefore, a shop is a good idea. We offer entrepreneurs space within the limits of Veere's livability. A campsite does not always fit.

125446.explanation: For a sustainable municipality, it is important that green initiatives are supported. This support can, for example, consist of help with applying for permits or setting up a foundation.

125448.explanation: D66 takes responsibility. The municipality of Veere already contributes in places like Serooskerke and with the accommodation of Ukrainians. Small-scale shelter is encouraged.

125449.explanation: We make it easier for employers to arrange housing for employees on their own premises. This housing must, of course, comply with all applicable rules for safe and healthy homes. This leaves more houses available for people who work here, and for young and elderly people looking for a home.

125453.explanation: Because the municipality consists of 13 villages, such a show is only interesting for a small part of the residents. That is not proportional to the costs and the environmental impact.

125454.explanation: We want to take big steps in waste policy as D66. We actually produced more residual waste in 2024. Therefore, we embrace the reverse collection system that the municipality – on D66's initiative – will introduce from 2027. Having bulky waste collected at home actually costs money.

125455.explanation: Events make our municipality attractive, lively, and offer residents the chance to meet each other. As far as D66 is concerned, our municipality should also be vibrant on Sundays. It is the municipality's role to support initiators and entrepreneurs in putting their ideas into practice.

125457.explanation: Vacant farms and sheds are excellent for residential care farms, especially near the centers. Own initiatives for housing, such as collective private commissioning, can count on D66's support. The municipality maintains control.

125461.explanation: The municipality covers the basic facilities, but the implementation is up to the association. D66 encourages cooperation between associations.

125463.explanation: There are too few enforcement officers employed to check whether residents, visitors, and businesses comply with municipal rules, such as parking, building regulations, rental rules, environmental permits, and environmental regulations. This makes the municipality a toothless tiger. We need to catch up on the backlog.

125464.explanation: Camera surveillance can contribute to increasing the feeling of safety. At the same time, cameras also infringe on the privacy of the law-abiding majority. Cameras are therefore only used where absolutely necessary and not longer than needed.

125469.explanation: We want to enable permanent residence in second homes, summer houses, and holiday homes now that the housing market is overheated. We make this possible for five to seven years, with the option to extend.

125470.explanation: D66 wants to use free plots of land within the centers for housing. Because D66 prefers to build new homes within the centers. Infill development therefore takes precedence over expansion of village centers. At the same time, expansion should also be possible. The challenges in housing are too great.

125471.explanation: Every resident contributes to society according to their ability and capacity, according to D66. Whether in paid work, informal care, or as a volunteer. We therefore regularly invite social assistance recipients to the town hall to actively seek a paid job or meaningful volunteer work.

125472.explanation: In case of debt problems, D66 intervenes as soon as possible to prevent deterioration of the situation. We want more attention for hidden poverty, for example among entrepreneurs. In poverty policy, D66 wants to do what is proven effective, including debt forgiveness where necessary.

125475.explanation: D66 wants to invest much more in charging stations within the municipality so that transport becomes cleaner. With parking lots outside the centers and alternative clean transport or rental bikes to the beaches, the municipality can also offer a real alternative in cooperation with market parties.

125476.explanation: If care or support is needed, it should align as much as possible with our residents. D66 believes in deciding together about what is needed. So not a unilateral decision by the municipality, but with the residents. Quality is more important than conviction.

125477.explanation: D66 wants to focus on the quality of youth care and will stop contracts with the lowest performing care providers. Well-performing care providers will receive long-term contracts so they can run their business sustainably. There is room for improvement in the procurement organization for youth care.

125478.explanation: Veere currently applies a relatively low rate compared to surrounding municipalities. There is no need to change that.

125480.explanation: The initiative proposal by D66 in January 2025 stated: Annual indexation like residents is only fair. The municipality has adopted this.

125481.explanation: D66 likes to remove unnecessary rules, for example opening hours. As far as D66 is concerned, our municipality should also be vibrant on Sundays. It is the municipality's task to support initiators and entrepreneurs in putting their ideas into practice.

125482.explanation: This is a legal obligation and includes 10% of the housing allocated. D66 believes the municipality must also keep this promise.

125484.explanation: D66 gladly gives space to entrepreneurs. It is up to the entrepreneur to decide whether agriculture should take place.

125488.explanation: Veere has a rich array of cultural heritage because each center has its own culture, dialect, and history. That heritage is unique and worth protecting and is addressed in schools. Dialect is learned at home, in the schoolyard, and at associations.

125489.explanation: If an application is submitted, we believe from social liberal principles that it must be assessed under the Environmental Law. We are not opposed to coffee shops.

125490.explanation: Veere must comply with the law, which includes 30%.

125491.explanation: The taboo on building more than two stories in new developments in Veere should be lifted, in our opinion. More living spaces for young people and elderly to promote flow are certainly possible by allowing higher than two stories. Up to 4 stories is acceptable.

125492.explanation: D66 sees merit in splitting large existing homes into multiple units, adding an extra independent living floor on top of existing houses, and more often inhabiting spaces above shops, restaurants, and other businesses. All this to address the housing shortage.

125493.explanation: Veere's parking policy should not be a disguised tax on our residents. D66 is therefore clear about parking fees: we use them to prevent congestion where necessary and mainly to have day tourists contribute to our facilities.

125496.explanation: We want to keep control over tourism growth, but stagnation is decline. An absolute ban is not good for the municipality as a whole, but uncontrolled growth neither. Livability is paramount.

125497.explanation: For the entire municipality, but especially for Aagtekerke and Grijpskerke, a solution must be found for traffic congestion within the centers. Excluding new asphalt beforehand is not wise.

125498.explanation: Money should be spent where it is needed and D66 trusts democratic principles.

125499.explanation: Creating care locations is primarily the responsibility of care providers, e.g., SVRZ. However, we support small-scale initiatives within the centers. These do not guarantee a place but deserve attention from care providers and the municipality.

125442.explanation: We attach great importance to representative democracy.

125445.explanation: Only if it supports agricultural operations.

125446.explanation: This depends on the plan. A revolving fund can be an incentive instrument.

125448.explanation: Good reception of refugees is our shared responsibility. Veere contributes fairly as part of Zeeland/Walcheren. Solutions should be small-scale and appropriate to Veere's scale.

125449.explanation: This is part of entrepreneurship and good employer practice.

125453.explanation: Our preference is for fireworks shows by clubs and associations under municipal supervision.

125454.explanation: Waste is a resource. Sorting at the recycling center is the norm to offer valuable raw materials for reuse. Only the collection of bulky household waste is free.

125455.explanation: Only when there is experienced nuisance.

125457.explanation: Building houses is an important task for the coming years. This requires careful allocation of space. The municipality explicitly has a role here. Within the set frameworks, self-managed housing development is an option.

125461.explanation: Sports practice is vital for health. Sports unite, promote a healthy lifestyle, and ensure people develop and are seen.

125463.explanation: Enforcement is about more than safety alone. Enforcement also fosters a sense of justice in society. Expanding enforcement capacity is possible when prioritization calls for it.

125464.explanation: Camera surveillance can help as a tool in tackling crime and nuisance. It is not a goal in itself. If applied, then proportionally and cautiously.

125469.explanation: Using holiday homes for permanent residence in the context of pre-informal care is a good thing, in our view. It can also be an option to help alleviate the housing shortage. But it must fit within local plans, with attention to safety, quality of life, and spatial planning.

125470.explanation: New construction should be possible adjacent to existing settlements.

125471.explanation: As part of a social and positive participation policy, in which everyone can participate according to their own abilities.

125472.explanation: This puts people's livelihood security under pressure.

125475.explanation: Based on the need and taking into account the growth forecast for electric driving.

125476.explanation: Preferably respect this choice, but due to personnel shortages in care provision, we must collaborate.

125477.explanation: Effectiveness and efficiency of youth care are also important criteria in the selection.

125478.explanation: The average housing costs in Veere are lower than the average in Zeeland.

125480.explanation: If necessary to maintain the standard of facilities in our municipality, an increase in tourist tax is acceptable.

125481.explanation: From our conviction, we enjoy Sunday as a collective day of rest. In many villages, Sunday rest has disappeared due to the rise of tourism. In some villages the shops are closed on Sundays. The Christian Union wants to keep it that way.

125482.explanation: The real problem lies in the very low number of social rental homes.

125484.explanation: First look for other options: solar panels on roofs.

125488.explanation: That is up to the primary schools themselves.

125489.explanation: Tolerance policies including allowing coffeeshops promote acceptance of drug use and encourage addiction.

125490.explanation: Only 10% of the total housing stock in Veere is social rental housing. This may be doubled.

125491.explanation: High-rise flats do not fit the rural character of the municipality. More residential floors than the current two with a roof is an option given the housing shortage.

125492.explanation: Given the housing shortage, every house counts. Renting rooms works against this.

125493.explanation: We find the current rates acceptable.

125496.explanation: Veere is an attractive place to spend a holiday. It should remain so. Our residents have a natural hospitality because they realize that tourism brings prosperity. There are also downsides: traffic problems, nuisance, and damage to vulnerable nature. Support is diminishing.

125497.explanation: The Christian Union favors widening existing roads and rerouting traffic flows to solve bottlenecks and unsafe situations. We oppose building new roads in our beautiful landscape.

125498.explanation: The challenges per village are diverse. This also translates into a different financial picture.

125499.explanation: Focusing on facilitating clustered living such as 'de Tienden' in Zoutelande, thereby optimally utilizing the strength of the local community.

125442.explanation: Having a say is good. Deciding along is better, so yes certainly referenda for BVNL!

125445.explanation: We make it easier for farmers to start a campsite or shop without unnecessary rules or procedures. For BVNL, farmers deserve opportunities, not bureaucracy.

125446.explanation: BVNL opposes financial support for collective energy initiatives by residents because public funds must be used efficiently and are not intended to subsidize private projects. Tax money is not a subsidy fund!

125448.explanation: BVNL opposes the establishment of an asylum seekers' center in the municipality of Veere because the impact on social cohesion, facilities, and safety is disproportionate. Veere is definitely not a reception location.

125449.explanation: BVNL believes companies are primarily responsible for accommodating their employees on their own premises. The municipality must facilitate this through clear and flexible procedures, preventing pressure on residential neighborhoods.

125453.explanation: No village or neighborhood excluded – fireworks belong to New Year's Eve. Whoever organizes this, it could be residents or the village council in consultation with police/fire brigade.

125454.explanation: BVNL believes the municipality should offer free bulky waste collection in all villages so residents are not unnecessarily burdened.

125455.explanation: BVNL believes that temporary exceedance of noise limits should be allowed at special events and festivals, with clear conditions and communication to local residents.

125457.explanation: BVNL supports the idea that farmers can build their own homes on their plots, provided this complies with spatial and environmental regulations and has no negative effects on the surroundings.

125461.explanation: BVNL believes the municipality can assist sports clubs facing temporary financial difficulties by temporarily waiving or reducing rent for their sports fields.

125463.explanation: Safety for residents comes first: more enforcement officers (BOA’s) should ensure we can guarantee this.

125464.explanation: BVNL believes that where camera surveillance is necessary, it must be implemented, with public safety outweighing individual privacy interests.

125469.explanation: No permanent residence in holiday homes – BVNL does not support this. A holiday home = vacation!

125470.explanation: BVNL states that building outside the core contours should be possible to promote housing construction, provided this is responsible and takes environmental, landscape, and infrastructure concerns into account.

125471.explanation: People receiving social assistance benefits must perform socially meaningful work in return, provided they are physically or mentally capable.

125472.explanation: BVNL states that waiving municipal taxes for low-income residents should be limited. The municipality should not grant loans and can at most waive a reduced amount.

125475.explanation: The municipality already does this. BVNL believes the municipality of Veere has sufficient charging points.

125476.explanation: BVNL believes clients should have the freedom to choose care providers who align with their faith, culture, or beliefs, provided these providers adhere to Dutch norms and values.

125477.explanation: BVNL believes the municipality should seek a balance between cost and quality when tendering youth care organizations, so that residents receive affordable and good care.

125478.explanation: BVNL believes that lowering the property tax is desirable because it reduces the financial burden on homeowners and contributes to an accessible housing climate. BVNL wants more room in people’s wallets!

125480.explanation: BVNL believes that the tourist tax should not be increased. The money collected must be fully spent on promoting and maintaining tourist facilities and activities. Tourists pay for tourism!

125481.explanation: BVNL believes that shopkeepers should have full freedom to determine their own opening days, without municipal restrictions on Sundays. Absolutely no top-down rules!

125482.explanation: According to BVNL, social rental homes should primarily be available for local residents. Refugees with residence permits should be placed at the back of the queue.

125484.explanation: No solar panels on farmland – our agricultural land is too valuable to use otherwise. Food production takes priority over energy – protect agricultural land!

125488.explanation: BVNL finds it important that primary schools offer lessons or activities about the Zeelandic dialect, similar to Frisian, in order to preserve cultural heritage and local identity.

125489.explanation: BVNL states that Veere should not issue permits for coffeeshops because this does not fit the desired municipal policy and living environment.

125490.explanation: BVNL believes that in new construction projects in Veere at least 30% of the homes should be developed as social housing to reduce the shortage of affordable housing. Increasingly more residents qualify for social or mid-level rental homes.

125491.explanation: BVNL states that high-rise flats are allowed in new construction projects in Veere, but this should be limited to a maximum of four residential floors to maintain balance in the streetscape and living environment.

125492.explanation: BVNL believes that landlords should have free choice to rent out rooms separately but that the municipality must ensure this does not result in homes functioning as student houses.

125493.explanation: BVNL states that parking in town centers should be cheaper and residents should have free parking options to make the center more accessible.

125496.explanation: BVNL states that the municipality should not issue permits for new holiday homes so existing village centers are preserved and sufficient housing remains available for permanent residents.

125497.explanation: BVNL states that laying new asphalt on the Northwest route should not be categorically forbidden. The municipality will carefully investigate this project before making decisions.

125498.explanation: BVNL states that municipal budgets must be fairly distributed across all centers and that the spending of these funds should be managed by the residents of the respective center.

125499.explanation: BVNL chooses: Care close to home: more money for care locations in the centers, together with housing associations and care institutions, so that elderly can continue living in their familiar surroundings.

statements.title: No more holiday homes may be added in the municipality of Veere.
The municipality should always allow farmers to open, for example, a shop or a campsite at their farm.
The municipality should make it possible for all primary schools to include attention to the Zeeuws dialect.
The residents of Veere should be able to express their opinion on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Veere moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: Veere should have more municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) focused on safety.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: Residents who want to generate sustainable energy together should receive financial support from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Energy based on resources that are effectively infinite, such as sunlight and wind.

titleParts.text: op willen wekken, moeten financiële steun krijgen van de gemeente.

statements.title: If sports clubs are struggling, the municipality should contribute financially.

titleParts.text: Als sportverenigingen het moeilijk hebben, moet de gemeente

titleParts.glossary: Something related to money.

statements.title: The municipality must allocate more money for care facilities in the villages.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet meer geld uittrekken voor zorglocaties in de

titleParts.glossary: Villages or small towns that are part of the municipality.

statements.title: People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: At least 30% of the new homes built in Veere should be social rental housing. Currently 28% of homes in the Netherlands are social rental housing.

titleParts.text: Van de nieuw te bouwen woningen in Veere moet minimaal 30% een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

titleParts.text: zijn. In Nederland is nu 28% van de woningen een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: The municipality should organize a fireworks show on New Year's Eve.
The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
High-rise residential buildings may be allowed in the municipality of Veere.
The tourist tax should be increased.

titleParts.glossary: The tax you must pay when you stay overnight in a hotel or at a campsite.

statements.title: Bulky waste must be collected from homes free of charge.
Veere should have more camera surveillance.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: Employers in agriculture and horticulture should provide housing for seasonal workers on their own premises.

titleParts.text: Werkgevers in de land- en tuinbouw moeten zelf zorgen voor de huisvesting van

titleParts.glossary: People who do work that only takes place during certain seasons, often in agriculture and fruit picking.

statements.title: Paid parking in the town centers should become cheaper.

titleParts.glossary: Villages or small towns that are part of the municipality.

statements.title: Each village/settlement should receive the same amount of money from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: A village or small town that is part of the municipality.

titleParts.text: moet evenveel geld krijgen van de gemeente.

statements.title: The municipality should ban solar panels on agricultural land.
People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: The municipality must allow holiday homes to be used as a permanent residence.
The municipality should stop forgiving municipal taxes for low-income residents.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet stoppen met het

titleParts.glossary: That people no longer have to pay for something.
Taxes you have to pay to the municipality (e.g., sewer charge, waste disposal charge, dog tax).

titleParts.text: voor inwoners met een laag inkomen.

statements.title: It should be possible for farmers to build houses themselves on their land.
The municipality must continue to give refugees with a residence permit priority for social housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: The municipality should prevent new construction outside existing village boundaries.
The municipality should allow the establishment of a coffeeshop.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van een

titleParts.glossary: Shop or café where you can buy and smoke soft drugs (hash, weed).

statements.title: The property tax (onroerendezaakbelasting, OZB) should be reduced.

titleParts.glossary: Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.
Property tax. Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.

statements.title: No new asphalt may be laid for the Noordwest Route.

shootoutStatements.title: Noise standards at festivals and parties must be made stricter.
An asylum seekers' center (AZC) should be established in our municipality.
The municipality must prohibit landlords from renting out separate rooms instead of whole houses.
The municipality must allow fewer Sunday shopping days.
When selecting organizations for youth care, the municipality should primarily pay attention to price.
